item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Your articles are the most encouraging and uplifting, which is a nice change of pace from the sort of existential dread a lot of articles rely on.

+1 forever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it. The interview is well done, and the article is spooky, ominous, somber. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: reminds me of SCP-1440 in a good way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank god something fresh and new to upvote. I can always count on folks like kaktus to provide something dope but this encourages me to look to the future with relatively new authors. Kudos.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has a Series 1 vibe in the best way possible. It's a beautiful article with a compelling character. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Extremely well done, I love the anomaly and it's personality.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A well done humanoid SCP is always a treat. The uneasy blend of self-awareness and compulsion on Hank's* part was well conveyed. And yes, the question of just what is breeding those baby horrors is a nice bit of Fridge Horror.

*I'm not going to call him 'it' and you can't make me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't have the words to express how charmed I am with this SCP. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the contrast between the quaint, folklore-y atmosphere and the extremely horrific corpse abominations he has a habit of wrangling. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fair reasoning and I apologize for my overreaction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like I should clarify my position a bit. My breaking point for interviews is reached when they are used to carry the SCP rather than enhance it, which seems to be not uncommon lately, souring me a bit on the use of them.

Maybe it's a quirk in my preferences, but IMO the core anomaly of an SCP should usually be the metaphorical cake, with interviews and other such stuff the icing. I'm probably not gonna eat a piece of stale toast slathered in a two-inch thick layer of icing.

Bringing this back to the article, it succeeds in capturing my interest long before we even get to the interview (good!) and the interview adds to the skip and doesn't feel like a waste of time/space (also good!).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And while I'm normally kinda sick of interviews…

This is the second time I've seen this opinion come up recently, and it's completely baffling to me. Of all the standard addenda formats we've had since the beginning, why single out interviews with sapient SCPs?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hank's character is fun, that description of TIAMAT-41 is a really cool monster design, and the last line of the interview was pretty clever. All in all a good read.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The last line spooked me, and it would make the people think who are the parents. Anyways I love how you told his story. Upvote for you author!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love the concept. The interview punchline made the cherry on top. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very neat concept. And while I'm normally kinda sick of interviews, Hank's character is fun enough to read that I enjoyed it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When I was reading this I was reminded of and idea for an SCP that I never have written about a fishing rod that allows you to catch impossible fish in any body of water. I guess now I probably will never write it because it would be too similar to this.

That last line in the interview really got me.

Here is a question. Is TIAMAT-41 a relative of SCP-2662?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liked it up until the nterview. Not gonna vote.

item.title: Re: oh boy oh gosh
item.content:encoded:
and thanks to you too, reader, for existing. ♥

How sweet. <3

It was a really nice read, and it had a bit of a backstory to Hank that was hinted throughout the article, especially with the final interview. That was a nice touch, and I feel like it singlehandedly added so much to the meaning of this document.

Very nice work!

item.title: oh boy oh gosh
item.content:encoded:
and thus i release my fourth creation: the force of nature known as Hank.

once again, thanks to everyone in chat that helped me brush this up and make it into a real catch.

and thanks to you too, reader, for existing. ♥
